Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: Course focuses on economic/fiscal issues and policy formation, directly supporting development-oriented policies for economic growth and productivity (Targets 8.1, 8.2, 8.3).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course's dedicated series on 'land planning issues' directly relates to developing sustainable and resilient infrastructure (Target 9.1).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: Course discusses public governance and economic/fiscal issues, which are key tools for adopting policies to achieve greater equality and social inclusion (Targets 10.2, 10.4).

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: The course's dedicated series on 'land planning issues' is fundamental to sustainable urbanization and human settlement planning (Targets 11.3, 11.a).

Climate Action.reason: No explicit or inferable evidence in the course description directly relates to climate action or climate change measures.

Life on Land.reason: The 'land planning issues' module could involve integrating ecosystem and biodiversity values into national planning (Target 15.9), though not explicitly stated.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course is fundamentally about public governance and policy formation, directly aiming to develop effective institutions and decision-making leaders (Targets 16.6, 16.7).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course explicitly invites 'industry, government, and academic experts,' directly reflecting multi-stakeholder partnerships (Target 17.17) and enhancing policy coherence.
